page: Microsoft Azure D16plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpldsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the Microsoft Neoverse-N2 CPU architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The processor configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 128 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a 945 GB local storage disk with Premium Storage capability. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in memory bandwidth and compression tasks. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth reached 307.54 GB/sec, and write bandwidth reached 124.58 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression registered at 33.08 GB/sec, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scored 176.1 GB/sec, also demonstrating strong tier performance. Conversely, CPU-centric benchmarks such as stress-ng single-core and multi-core, along with Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, fall within the average tier.

The server exhibits a clear tradeoff between high memory bandwidth and constrained memory capacity per core. While memory throughput and small-model LLM text generation perform strongly, prompt processing for larger 7B models is weak at 24.62 tokens/sec due to the 2.0 GB per core memory limitation. This hardware profile is suited for scale-out compute workloads, web serving, and lightweight containerized applications that benefit from dedicated ARM cores and local storage but do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios.
description: Microsoft Azure D16plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dpldsv6 family, powered by a 16-core Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 945 GB of local storage. The instance demonstrates strong performance in memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads at 307.54 GB/sec and writes at 124.58 GB/sec, alongside strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, CPU-centric benchmarks and Geekbench scores align with average tier performance. The low memory-to-CPU ratio presents a tradeoff, making it less suitable for memory-intensive databases but highly efficient for scale-out compute, web serving, and lightweight containerized workloads that leverage dedicated ARM64 architecture and local disk space.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16plds_v6 is a 16-core ARM64 virtual machine featuring a 3.4 GHz Neoverse-N2 processor, 32 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage, delivering strong memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16plds_v6 is a 16-core ARM64 virtual machine with 32 GB RAM and 945 GB local storage, optimized for scale-out compute workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores, high memory bandwidth, and local storage for compute-focused workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory and 945 GB of local storage
Strong memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression performance
Low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU
Suited for scale-out compute, web serving, and containerized workloads
